steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The students claim cataract frequency differences are solely attributable to Aasdh expression variation. To weaken this, a finding must show that Aasdh variation alone does not determine cataract frequency — i.e., different expression levels produce the same outcome.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Some wild-type mice were very similar to the knockout mice with regard to cataract frequency but showed a wide variety of levels of expression of *Aasdh*.: proves that varying Aasdh expression doesn't change the cataract outcome, directly undermining the 'solely attributable' claim. ✓
  • Choice B — The level of expression of *Aasdh* does not appear to affect the functioning of any other genes in house mice.: confirms the students' claim rather than weakening it. ✗
  • Choice C — A sampling of house mice captured in natural settings shows that individual mice can differ from one another in the level of expression of *Aasdh*.: shows variation is real but doesn't challenge whether it's the sole cause. ✗
  • Choice D — The mice with *Aasdh* deactivated were identical to the wild-type mice except with regard to cataract frequency.: makes the experiment more credible, which supports the conclusion. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
